items.title: ACCC blocks Coles supermarket in Kalgoorlie to protect regional city's independents

children.content: In an unprecedented move, Australia's consumer watchdog exercises new powers to thwart the opening of a Coles supermarket in the WA outback city, fearing it could knock out smaller competitors in the wider region.

items.title: Alcoa to buy South32's WA bauxite mine in $8b deal

image.altText: Massive industrial crusher surrounded by strip mined land and red dirt

children.content: American mining giant Alcoa is set to buy $8 billion worth of assets from Australia's South32, including the major bauxite mine and alumina refinery near Boddington in WA's Peel region.

items.title: BHP shows off two electric trucks — with 62 diesel ones on order

children.content: BHP and Rio Tinto have put their two battery-electric trucks on show in Western Australia's Pilbara, but is an aversion to risk stifling meaningful change?

items.title: When working more hours might mean you lose your home

children.content: Fremantle resident Trish Owen could lose the tenancy on her government-provided home if she works more hours — and a new study suggests many people are in the same situation.

items.title: Tough new 'fit and proper person' tests for WA tow truck operators

children.content: The new laws come into effect from today, including "fit and proper person" tests for business operators.

image.altText: A tow truck sits on a flatbed truck.

items.title: Photos show room where police allege Thai teenager was last seen

children.content: New photographs reveal the cluttered room where 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was allegedly murdered by Australian man Simon Peter Carman, who moved to Thailand from WA late last year.

articles.title: Man accused of Thai girl's murder grew up and owned firearms in WA, moved eight months ago

children.content: Australian Simon Peter Carman went about "normal life", going out, washing his clothes and talking to neighbours after allegedly murdering a teenage girl and stuffing her body inside a suitcase in Thailand, police say.

items.title: Iconic Gloucester Tree reopens after three-year closure

children.content: Tourism operators are celebrating the reopening of the southern WA landmark, the Gloucester Tree, with visitors making the 37-metre climb to its peak for the first time since its closure three years ago.

image.altText: A low angle shot of a tree with spiral planks leading up to the very top of the tree and its leaves

items.title: 'Fear and panic' as more NDIS registration rules loom

children.content: Kalgoorlie mother Jo Russell and her daughter Megan are among the families concerned about changes to the NDIS provider sector coming into effect today.

image.altText: An older woman holding her adult daughter in a park

items.title: Family appeals for help to find Perth man missing in Mexico for almost a month

children.content: Jesse Bruzzese's family say the 35-year-old solo traveller has not been in touch with them since arriving in Mexico at the beginning of June.

items.title: Whale census records unexplained drop in sightings off WA's South West

children.content: Marine scientists have expressed concerns following a 60 per cent drop in migrating whales seen off South West WA's coastline compared to previous years.

items.title: PM sidesteps question on whether house prices will continue falling

children.content: Anthony Albanese has sidestepped questions on whether house prices will continue falling, following the release of data today showing a 0.4 per cent drop in home values. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese raises his hand while speaking in parliament.

items.title: Paediatrician accused of child sex offences now facing 53 charges

children.content: A Perth paediatrician accused of using hidden cameras to record himself abusing children is now facing more than 50 charges involving 10 alleged victims, with a court told further charges are likely to be laid.

image.altText: A head and shoulders shot of an Asian-Australian man, with black hair, wearing spectacles and a blue and white business shirt.

items.title: Quick hits: Mexico madness at the Azteca, big Salah injury update

children.content: Mexico fans enjoy a night to remember at the Azteca, Egypt gives a big update on Mo Salah's fitness, and the Netherlands lose their manager after a shootout disaster. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

articles.title: 'No tomorrow' mindset for Socceroos ahead of World Cup clash with Egypt

children.content: The Socceroos' World Cup adventure will either end or become historic when they face Egypt on Friday.

image.altText: Jordan Bos of Australia steals the ball from Julio Enciso of Paraguay

articles.title: French unity on display as their ruthless run through World Cup continues

children.content: In a French team littered with superstars it is something intangible that could stop them taking another World Cup title, following another clinical display against Sweden in the round of 32.

items.title: Liberals can't keep on track even with a prize in sight

children.content: The Liberals, it seems, can't resist returning to damaging infighting, even in the one part of the country where things have been going quite encouragingly for them.

image.altText: Moira Deeming appears serious as she walks through the parliament carpark, wearing a purple blazer.

items.title: ASX dips, while AI-buying frenzy drives Dow to record high

children.content: The Australian share market eases slightly, even as an AI stock-led rally boosted the Dow Jones to a record high for a second straight day. Follow live.

items.title: Call for law change after comedian's 'terrifying' roadside assistance ordeal

children.content: Comedian Laura Johnston calls for changes to the law after saying she was confronted by an NRMA contractor who could not provide identification, behaved aggressively and swore at her before leaving her stranded in remote NSW.

items.title: Why Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams before their match

children.content: Australian Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams in the days leading up to their Wimbledon opener just to remove some of the mythos around the 23-time grand slam champion.

image.altText: Serena Williams and Maya Joint walk onto centre court at Wimbledon.

items.title: The condition that causes people to get lost in their own home

children.content: Some estimates suggest up to one in 30 people may be affected by a condition that has been described as a lifelong inability to orient oneself — even in extremely familiar surroundings.

image.altText: A man stands in a stairwell

items.title: Certain sports have a high risk of brain injury. What can you do as a parent?

children.content: When your child decides they are keen on playing sport, there are lots of factors to consider as a parent, and after recent news stories about chronic traumatic encephalopathy, keeping them safe from head injuries may be front of mind.

image.altText: A group of teens wearing team soccer jerseys celebrate on the field.

items.title: 'Recurring issues' uncovered following surveillance of chopper sector

children.content: Australia's aviation watchdog has warned that cutting corners on maintenance and certification requirements could create a "real safety risk".

image.altText: A chopper shot from below, it is parked on green grass and a blue sky can be seen in the background.

items.title: Bronze tugboat propellers worth $2m stolen in WA's north

children.content: In a heist not seen before in this WA mining town, thieves have allegedly made away with eight tonnes of tugboat propellers, with the copper estimated to be worth an eye-watering $2 million.

gallery.altText: Bronze propellers in yellow frames in an industrial yard.
